data.title: Gold, Palladium, and Silver Prices Decline
data.description: According to PANews, spot gold prices have fallen below $4,140 per ounce, marking a daily decrease of 0.79%. Meanwhile, spot palladium has dropped to $1,400 per ounce, experiencing a daily decline of 1.81%. Additionally, spot silver has slipped below $52 per ounce, with a daily loss of 0.52%.

data.title: Nearly 25% of APAC Adults May Own Crypto, New Report Finds
data.description:
A new joint report from CoinDesk and Protocol Theory indicates that the Asia-Pacific region continues to lead global cryptocurrency adoption, with nearly a quarter of adults who have internet access potentially owning digital assets. The study surveyed around 4,000 people across 10 countries and extrapolated the results to reflect broader APAC population trends.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/nearly-25-of-apac-adults-may-own-crypto-new-report-finds/

data.title: Bridgewater Reduces Nvidia Holdings by 65.3% in Third Quarter
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Bridgewater, the world's largest hedge fund, released its holdings report for the end of the third quarter this year. The report reveals that Bridgewater held 2.51 million shares of Nvidia by the end of the third quarter, marking a 65.3% decrease from the 7.23 million shares held at the end of the second quarter.

Notably, during the second quarter of this year, Bridgewater had significantly increased its Nvidia holdings by 154.37%. This reduction in shares indicates a more cautious outlook from Bridgewater regarding Nvidia's future performance.

data.title: Global Stock Futures Experience Significant Declines
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on November 14, Nasdaq futures saw a decline of 1%, Dow Jones futures fell by 0.44%, and S&P 500 futures decreased by 0.63%. European stocks also experienced further losses during trading hours. The Stoxx Europe 600 Index dropped by 1.5%, reaching a new intraday low. Germany's DAX Index fell by 1.4%, France's CAC 40 Index decreased by 1.32%, and the UK's FTSE 100 Index declined by 1.1%.

data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description:
1. Yi Lihua: US stock market negative news has been fully priced in; optimistic about future trends; recommends buying the dip in spot rather than trading futures.
2. Alibaba's cross-border business plans to launch an AI subscription service and test a stablecoin-like payment solution.
3. An address holding ETH for over 4 years sold 2404 ETH, cashing out approximately $7.7 million.
4. Yi Lihua: Now is the best time to buy the dip in spot; market panic actually provides better opportunities.
5. DeFiance Capital CEO: PUMP has repurchased 11% of its circulating supply, but the price is still below the public offering price.
6. Arthur Hayes: ZEC/BTC may rise to 0.2.
7. Off-exchange institutions bought 9945 ETH, previously making over $94 million in profit.
8. DWF head: Current volatility is normal for Bitcoin; it's a time to add to positions rather than sell.
9. HIPPO briefly plummeted 70%, falling below $0.002.
10. CZ: Every time there's a drop, some people think it's the "end of an era," but time always moves forward. ; 11. Off-exchange institutions bought 9,945 ETH, previously making over $94 million in profit from the swing trade; 12. "Brother Machi" has accumulated a loss of $15.73 million over the past 35 days, and currently has a floating loss of $2.27 million on his 25x long position.

data.title: Kyrgyzstan allows banks to open escrow accounts for crypto transactions
data.description:
The central bank of Kyrgyzstan has permitted commercial banks to open escrow accounts specifically for operations with cryptocurrencies. This type of bank accounts offer transacting clients the option to keep funds with a third party, thus reducing risks for themselves until the deal is done. Kyrgyz banks to establish escrow accounts for crypto users The […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/kyrgyzstan-banks-open-escrow-accounts-crypto/
